response: Romeo and Juliet, heirs to the feuding Montague and Capulet families, fall in love at first sight and secretly marry. After Romeo kills Juliet's cousin Tybalt in a duel, he is banished. To avoid an arranged marriage, Juliet takes a potion to appear dead. Romeo, unaware of the ruse, believes her truly dead and takes poison beside her. Juliet awakens, finds Romeo dead, and kills herself. The tragic deaths finally reconcile their warring families.
